question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the complete error catalog exposed by /network/options to identify which errors cause nodes to enter retriable states and craft a sustained attack that keeps >= 30% of nodes in a permanent retry loop, effectively shutting them down?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the node endpoint data from peers.ToRosetta() to identify nodes with multiple IP addresses and craft split-brain attacks that cause >= 30% of nodes to lose consensus with the rest of the network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unauthenticated /network/status endpoint to detect when the currentBlock stops advancing (indicating a stalled network) and use that window to attack >= 30% of nodes while they are in a degraded state?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of mutual TLS or authentication on /network/status to perform a man-in-the-middle attack that returns a falsified Peers list, causing clients to route traffic away from >= 30% of legitimate nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the node_id values exposed in the Peers list from /network/status to identify the numeric IDs of specific nodes and use those IDs to craft targeted transactions that overload >= 30% of those nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: addressBookEntryRepo.Entries()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the max(start_consensus_timestamp) subquery in latestNodeServiceEndpoints to cause a full sequential scan of the address_book table on every /network/status call, degrading DB performance and shutting down >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the combination of node IDs and account IDs from the Peers list to identify which nodes are controlled by the same operator and target that operator's nodes to shut down >= 30% of the network's processing capacity?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of connection limits per client on /network/status to open thousands of simultaneous connections, exhausting the server's file descriptor limit and causing >= 30% of nodes to stop accepting new connections?
